name: Fajitas
recipeIngredient: One 8-oz bottle herbs and garlic oil-based
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat pit.
2. Mix all ingredients except beef together to make a marinade.
3. Pour over meat in a non-reactive container. Cover and stir occasionally for 6-8 hours prior to cooking.
4. Cook the fajitas either of these ways:
a. If you have the space, smoke the fajitas for about 30 minutes with pure mesquite smoke, then cook over direct heat (mesquite coals are best) for about 4-7 minutes per side. Baste with marinade while cooking.
b. Alternatively, cook over direct heat (mesquite coals are best) for 6-10 minutes per side until desired doneness is reached. Baste with marinade while cooking.
5. Immediately slice the meat at a 45-degree diagonal cut across the grain of the meat.
6. Serve with guacamole and pico de gallo in warm flour tortillas. Plan for 1/2 pound of meat and 3-4 flour tortillas per person.
